text: A full-time worker should be able to afford basic housing and food, regardless of the market value of their skills.
A reliable bureaucracy and well-maintained public infrastructure are more important to daily life than rapid political change.
The government should not restrict the publication of false or offensive information on the internet.
Essential resources like water and electricity should be guaranteed to all residents regardless of their ability to pay.
A society functions best when citizens prioritize long-term institutional continuity over their own immediate gratification.
Adults should be legally permitted to engage in high-risk financial investments or gambling without state safety nets.
The fact that a child's economic future is heavily predicted by their parents' wealth is a flaw in the system that needs correction.
Immigrants should be expected to fully assimilate into the dominant culture and language of their new country.
A property owner should have the unrestricted right to develop their land as they see fit, regardless of neighborhood zoning or environmental impact.
If a free market naturally produces severe inequality, the system is broken and must be heavily regulated.
Neighborhoods have the right to preserve their historical character and architecture, even if it limits new development.
If you prefer to invest your own money, you should be legally allowed to opt out of government pension and healthcare programs.
Workers should have a democratic share of ownership and decision-making in the companies they help build.
The primary goal of the penal system should be to isolate dangerous individuals to protect the public, not to attempt to rehabilitate them.
Adults should be legally permitted to sell their own organs for money if they consent to the procedure.
If a tenant stops paying rent due to a severe medical emergency, the state should legally prevent the landlord from evicting them.
Selective public universities should base admissions entirely on academic test scores, ignoring demographic or socioeconomic backgrounds.
The possession and sale of recreational drugs should be treated entirely as a public market issue rather than a criminal justice issue.
Protests that block major roadways are a legitimate tactic to bring attention to pressing social issues.
Cities should strictly enforce curfews and noise ordinances, even if it severely limits the nightlife and cultural expression of young adults.
An employer should have the absolute legal right to terminate workers who attempt to unionize on company time.
During a natural disaster, hardware stores must be legally prohibited from raising prices on essential items like water and generators.
Parents should be able to pass all their accumulated wealth to their children without the state extracting any inheritance tax.
Occupational licenses for jobs like hair braiding or interior design should be abolished so people can more easily start their own businesses.
To address public health crises, the state is justified in banning the advertisement of highly processed, high-sugar foods to children.
If a person voluntarily buys a home in a private community, the homeowner's association should have the absolute authority to dictate aesthetic choices to maintain neighborhood harmony.
Developers should be allowed to build high-density apartment complexes in single-family neighborhoods to increase housing supply, regardless of local opposition.
If a specific online forum consistently hosts medical misinformation that demonstrably harms public health, the state should shut it down.
Property developers should be exempt from local procedural vetoes and community lawsuits if they are building critical infrastructure like power grids.
The government should never use taxpayer money to bail out a failing corporation, regardless of how many jobs will be lost or the economic shock.
Landlords should be legally required to accept tenants using government housing vouchers if the tenant meets all standard background criteria.
Assume a specific welfare program is definitively proven to encourage long-term generational dependency; it should be cut, even if it temporarily leaves some citizens in poverty.
People should be allowed to try unapproved, experimental medical treatments if they are out of options, bypassing all bureaucratic safety regulations.
Public school funding should be pooled at the state level and distributed equally per student, removing local neighborhood funding control entirely.
The government should strictly limit foreign imports to protect domestic industries, even if it raises prices for everyday consumers.
If a tech company naturally outcompetes rivals and controls a market without fraud, violence, or direct government favoritism, the state should leave them alone.
We should enforce high taxes on top earners even if it causes the most highly skilled doctors and engineers to emigrate.
A society functions best when citizens prioritize loyalty to their family and local community over their own personal ambitions.
If an adult makes a series of terrible financial decisions and loses everything, society has no obligation to rescue them with taxpayer money.
Cities should redesign streets to prioritize public transit and pedestrians, even if it significantly increases commute times for drivers.
If a city cannot afford its utility budget, it is acceptable to shut off water to households that are several months behind on their bills.
If a historic, privately owned building falls into severe disrepair, the owner should be allowed to demolish it to build a modern structure.
It is necessary to aggressively reform or dismantle long-standing institutions if they consistently produce unequal outcomes.
It is acceptable for the government to aggressively monitor private communications if it demonstrably prevents organized crime and terrorism.
If an adult voluntarily accepts employment at a wage rate below the standard cost of living, the state should not intervene to override that agreement.
